text.title: Laravel Layered Patterns (Controller → Service → Resource) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Laravel codebases.

antipattern.symptom: OrderController.php grows to 600 lines; new feature requires modifying 4 different methods; bugs leak into unrelated endpoints.
antipattern.root-cause: No service layer; all logic + validation + tx + response shaping live in controller.
antipattern.fix: Extract service per concern; controller becomes 5-10 lines per action.

antipattern.symptom: Store + Update endpoints repeat the same 15 validation rules; rules drift between endpoints.
antipattern.fix: Generate StoreOrderRequest + UpdateOrderRequest; both extend BaseOrderRequest with shared rules in protected method.

antipattern.symptom: API returns timestamps + soft-delete flag + internal foreign keys; client integrations break on schema migration.
antipattern.fix: Wrap in OrderResource with explicit field allowlist; version the resource (V1, V2).

antipattern.symptom: Two controllers have different rules for who can edit an order; security bug shipped.
antipattern.fix: Create OrderPolicy; register; call `$this->authorize('update', $order)` in controller __construct via authorizeResource.
